title: Computer Numerically Controlled Tool Operators
description: Operate computer-controlled tools, machines, or robots to machine or process parts, tools, or other work pieces made of metal, plastic, wood, stone, or other materials. May also set up and maintain equipment.

tasks.text: Measure dimensions of finished workpieces to ensure conformance to specifications, using precision measuring instruments, templates, and fixtures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up and operate computer-controlled machines or robots to perform one or more machine functions on metal or plastic workpieces.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Mount, install, align, and secure tools, attachments, fixtures, and workpieces on machines, using hand tools and precision measuring instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review program specifications or blueprints to determine and set machine operations and sequencing, finished workpiece dimensions, or numerical control sequences.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Stop machines to remove finished workpieces or to change tooling, setup, or workpiece placement, according to required machining sequences.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Listen to machines during operation to detect sounds such as those made by dull cutting tools or excessive vibration, and adjust machines to compensate for problems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Implement changes to machine programs, and enter new specifications, using computers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Calculate machine speed and feed ratios and the size and position of cuts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Transfer commands from servers to computer numerical control (CNC) modules, using computer network links.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove and replace dull cutting tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check to ensure that workpieces are properly lubricated and cooled during machine operation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adjust machine feed and speed, change cutting tools, or adjust machine controls when automatic programming is faulty or if machines malfunction.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor machine operation and control panel displays, and compare readings to specifications to detect malfunctions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Maintain machines and remove and replace broken or worn machine tools, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Insert control instructions into machine control units to start operation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Modify cutting programs to account for problems encountered during operation, and save modified programs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Write simple programs for computer-controlled machine tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lift workpieces to machines manually or with hoists or cranes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Input initial part dimensions into machine control panels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up future jobs while machines are operating.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with supervisors or programmers to resolve machine malfunctions or production errors or to obtain approval to continue production.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Stack or load finished items, or place items on conveyor systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Control coolant systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean machines, tooling, or parts, using solvents or solutions and rags.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Enter commands or load control media, such as tapes, cards, or disks, into machine controllers to retrieve programmed instructions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lay out and mark areas of parts to be shot peened and fill hoppers with shot.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine electronic components for defects or completeness of laser-beam trimming, using microscopes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
